input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule V.3.2.10 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: v.3.2.10
            TITLE: rear wheel steering may be used.

            CONTENT:
            rule V.3.2.10: Rear wheel steering may be used.
V.3.2.10 Rear wheel steering may be used.   
a. Rear wheel steering must incorporate mechanical stops to limit the range of angular 
movement of the rear wheels to a maximum of six degrees (6°).  
b. The team must provide the ability for the steering angle range to be verified at Technical 
Inspection with a driver in the vehicle.  
c. Rear wheel steering may be electrically actuated.

final_answer: rule V.3.2.10: Rear wheel steering may be used. V.3.2.10 Rear wheel steering may be used. a. Rear wheel steering must incorporate mechanical stops to limit the range of angular movement of the rear wheels to a maximum of six degrees (6°). b. The team must provide the ability for the steering angle range to be verified at Technical Inspection with a driver in the vehicle. c. Rear wheel steering may be electrically actuated.
